variant: give me the numbers on sleep and muscle growth, bold each stat

request.message: give me the numbers on sleep and muscle growth, bold each stat

response_body.response:
here are the sleep and muscle growth numbers from your protocol: **7–9 hours** is the evidence-based target for optimal recovery and hypertrophy. **~70–80% of daily growth hormone** is released during slow-wave sleep (within the first 2 hours of sleep). sleep deprivation under **6 hours per night** reduces testosterone by **10–15%** within a week and suppresses GH pulse amplitude.

a **60-minute sleep wind-down** measurably improves slow-wave sleep quality. room temperature **65–68°f (18–20°c)** is linked to better deep-sleep architecture.

on the muscle side: **0.25–0.5 lb per week** (0.5–2 lb per month) for intermediate lifters; beginners can hit **1–2 lb per month** in the first year. **muscle protein synthesis** stays elevated for **24–48 hours** post-training and peaks **4–6 hours** after a session. hit **25–40 g protein per meal** to maximize per-meal MPS.

train each muscle **10–20 hard sets per week**; growth drops off past **20 sets** for most people.

products.name: Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard 100% Whey Protein
products.description: 24g whey isolate per scoop — third-party tested, low-cost per gram.

cards.label: daily growth hormone released during slow-wave sleep
protein per meal for max MPS
weekly volume sweet spot per muscle
